subject: grammar
title: Collocations: Adjectives and Verbs
instruction: Fill in the gaps in the conversation with a word from the box and a preposition.
passage:
Teacher: Do you think that money can solve all problems?
Student: No, not really. Of course, I would be 1 anxious about (anxious about) money if I didn't have very much, but I believe that too much money can be 2 [2] you.
Teacher: So you think it is possible to have too much money?
Student: Well, there are lots of stories in the media about people who have won lots of money in the lottery and realised that they are not happier as a result. I think that large amounts of money can have a negative 3 [3] people.
Teacher: Do you think that people 4 [4] modern technology like computers too much and that people are 5 [5] having the latest high tech equipment?
Student: Well, yes, in some countries. But this isn't 6 [6] many people in the world who are only 7 [7] their next meal. In rich countries people always want a new gadget to make life easier but I'm not sure these machines do actually do that. When we go shopping we have to 8 [8] hundreds of different brands of the same thing, and when these things go wrong we don't know what to do. I would prefer it if life were simpler and we didn't 9 [9] expensive technologies so much.
